item.title: Nissan shareholders vote out director who backed merger with Honda
item.description: Former Mizuho executive Motoo Nagai fails to receive approval for reappointment after Renault abstains

item.title: FirstFT: EU waits for Burnham
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: ChatGPT ads and EU deportation centres

item.title: AI Exchange
item.description: In this monthly series, running alongside our existing Tech Exchange dialogues, FT journalists talk to the scientists, developers and business leaders exploring ever more applications for artificial intelligence in every aspect of our lives

item.title: US and Iran give shipowners conflicting Hormuz orders
item.description: Vessels given incompatible instructions on safest route to cross strait

item.title: Private equity’s new vibe check
item.description: Buyout investors are recreating software products to gauge their competitiveness

item.title: OpenAI pitches ChatGPT ads to Cannes marketers ahead of IPO
item.description: Lossmaking AI group is presenting at Cannes Lions advertising conference for the first time

item.title: Getty Images shows the inimitable value of an OpenAI photobomb
item.description: Having fought vigorously to defend its copyright, the picture agency is trying a new approach

item.title: Private equity-owned vet group IVC spends £34mn on UK competition probe
item.description: Conclusion of industry-wide investigation by Competition and Markets Authority paves way for IPO

item.title: Private equity bosses turn to carried interest loans as payouts stall
item.description: Requests to borrow against future shares of profits are multiplying, as the buyout market remains slow

item.title: New York’s green energy rethink is a lesson for the UK
item.description: AI data centres and electricity shortages are forcing the state to weaken its opposition to fossil fuels

item.title: Citadel: the hedge fund that became an energy giant
item.description: From the ashes of Enron, Ken Griffin built a commodities empire that extends beyond financial trading

item.title: How Europe’s refineries helped save your summer holiday
item.description: Plants pushed jet fuel production to record levels after Iran war cut off Middle East export routes

item.title: Top carmakers warn EU tech sovereignty drive will raise costs
item.description: Brussels’ proposals to cut reliance on US Big Tech spark concerns among European carmakers

item.title: Nvidia’s Kimberly Powell: We are reinventing the doctor experience
item.description: The chipmaker’s head of healthcare argues AI can ease many of the sector’s ills, including reducing medics’ workload and tackling the shortage of trained staff

item.title: An AI venture soared as it touted links to Trump. Now it’s fighting for survival
item.description: Shares in Fermi collapsed after it failed to secure an anchor tenant and its CEO was fired. Can it ever fulfil its promise?

item.title: South Koreans pour AI stock windfalls into overheated property market
item.description: Government struggles to convince investors to shift money away from real estate despite tech rally

item.title: Indonesia stock market reforms could prompt delistings
item.description: Analysts warn some companies may leave rather than abide by larger free-float requirements and greater transparency

item.title: Eli Lilly deploys weight-loss cash on ‘App Store’ for scientists
item.description: Mounjaro maker is collaborating with small biotechs on AI as a tool for drug discovery

item.title: Netherlands to open up €1.5tn slice of pensions market to foreign providers
item.description: Domestic funds dominate country’s workplace retirement system

item.title: KPMG Australia’s chair and senior partners to depart in wake of audit scandal
item.description: Firm set to appoint independent chair as part of ‘action plan’

item.title: Mukesh Ambani points to succession plan for Reliance Industries
item.description: Also in this newsletter, Modi’s great hopes for Indian arms manufacturing

item.title: Apollo’s flagship private credit fund hit by 17% redemption requests
item.description: Vehicle meets less than a third of withdrawal requests as investor exodus accelerates

item.title: Hedge fund to pay Avis $650mn in settlement after wild stock swings
item.description: Pentwater Capital rapidly sold shares in US car rental group as price soared then plummeted

item.title: SpaceX sheds $400bn in market value as debut rally hits reverse
item.description: Shares in Elon Musk’s AI and rockets group tumble more than 16% following fresh rise in US bond yields

item.title: CRH digs deep for a bigger share of the US construction boom
item.description: Irish building materials group’s deal for Arcosa is worth doing

item.title: EasyJet should hold out for a tad more from Castlelake
item.description: UK budget airline has minimal debt and is expected to grow operating profit sevenfold from this year’s depressed level

item.title: Indian entrepreneur to lead WhatsApp as Zuckerberg brings in new blood
item.description: Fintech founder Kunal Shah to replace Will Cathcart who steps down after seven years leading the Meta-owned messaging app

item.title: EasyJet and its investors hold out for £600mn more from Castlelake
item.description: Shareholders call for private credit group’s £4.7bn approach to be increased to at least £7-a-share

item.title: Chevron moves into power production with Microsoft AI deal
item.description: Company signs 20-year agreement to develop data centre in heart of US oil country that could include gas-fired plant

item.title: AI-powered threats may succeed ‘within months’, Five Eyes warns
item.description: Western governments and corporates cautioned that their lead in AI might not last long
